title: Firefox doesn't load any URL with --cap-drop all
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Custom Firefox container fails to load any URL when started with **--cap-drop all**. I've had this working previously, but I'm not sure if it's Podman related at all, since it's been a while I have last tried this. IIRC I had this working before the capabilities issues that 2.2.0 solved. Probably with 1.9.2.

The below were the commands I used in order to create a firefox-test container which exhibits this behaviour:

$ buildah from --name tmp docker.io/library/debian:latest
$ buildah run tmp sh -c "apt update && apt install --no-install-recommends -y firefox-esr ttf-dejavu && rm -fr /var/lib/apt/lists/*"
$ buildah run tmp useradd -m -s /bin/bash firefox-esr
$ buildah config -u firefox-esr tmp
$ buildah config --entrypoint firefox-esr tmp
$ buildah commit tmp firefox-test
$ buildah rm tmp

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. sudo podman run --cap-drop all --rm -v /tmp:/tmp -e DISPLAY=:0 firefox-test

**Describe the results you received:**

Firefox fails to render the typed URL. As soon as enter is pressed, the window looks like it enters a loop and nothing is displayed. The main Firefox windows is still responsive, though.

**Describe the results you expected:**

Successful render of the typed URL.

**Output of `podman version`:**

Version:      2.2.1
API Version:  2.1.0
Go Version:   go1.15.6
Git Commit:   a0d478edea7f775b7ce32f8eb1a01e75374486cb
Built:        Tue Dec  8 21:48:23 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64

comments.body: Looks like some other security mechanism is causing the issue.  If you don't drop all calls, does it work?
I would also think you need to share the network with the host for this to work.
@rhatdan, thanks for helping! That is correct, without --cap-drop is works flawlessly. By sharing the network, do you mean --net host? Why would that have any influence over this?
Just tried with **--cap-drop all** and **--net host** and it has the same effect, sadly.

I guess the interesting thing, is what cap does it need.  I would figure DAC_OVERRIDE?

--cap-drop=all --cap-add override

And see if this works.

Same thing, @rhatdan. I'll try to see what capability makes it work.
I'm not seeing anything particularly interesting with dmesg:

Cheers!
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

On Fedora 33, this works fine for me.
$ cat /tmp/Containerfile 
FROM fedora
RUN yum -y install firefox
$ podman build -t firefox /tmp
...
$ podman run -ti --security-opt label=disable --rm -v /tmp:/tmp -e DISPLAY=:0 firefox /usr/bin/firefox

Firefox window shows up.

I had to disable SELinux separation.  But everything else worked. 

I can also drop all caps except DAC_OVERRIDE, and it works.

$ podman run -ti --cap-drop=all --cap-add dac_override --security-opt label=disable --rm -v /tmp:/tmp -e DISPLAY=:0 firefox /usr/bin/firefox 

I have not tried this as root, but I am pretty sure it would work.
